name: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 — Requirements engineering (life cycle processes)
summary: Defines the characteristics of a good requirement SET (complete, consistent, feasible, comprehensible, able to be validated, bounded) and the content topics of a Software Requirements Specification. Used here as the meta-quality oracle for whether the requirement set is well-formed, plus the SRS section topics that should be present.

leaves.name: Complete (as a set)
leaves.description: The set of requirements needs no further amplification — it covers everything necessary with no omissions.

leaves.name: Consistent
leaves.description: The set contains no conflicting or contradictory requirements and uses consistent terminology.

leaves.name: Feasible / affordable
leaves.description: The set can be satisfied within known constraints — cost, schedule, technology and risk.

leaves.name: Comprehensible
leaves.description: The set is understandable by the stakeholders who must use it, with clear structure and unambiguous language.

leaves.name: Able to be validated
leaves.description: It is possible to demonstrate the set of requirements collectively delivers the needs, via verifiable fit criteria.

leaves.name: Bounded
leaves.description: The set keeps to the scope necessary to meet the need — no scope creep or out-of-scope items.

leaves.name: SRS: functions
leaves.description: SRS section specifying the required functions/capabilities of the system or software.

leaves.name: SRS: performance requirements
leaves.description: SRS section specifying static and dynamic performance requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: usability requirements
leaves.description: SRS section specifying usability and human-factors requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: interface requirements
leaves.description: SRS section specifying external interfaces — user, hardware, software and communications interfaces.

leaves.name: SRS: data / logical database requirements
leaves.description: SRS section specifying data entities, logical database and information the system stores and manages.

leaves.name: SRS: design constraints
leaves.description: SRS section specifying design and implementation constraints — standards compliance, mandated technology, physical limits.

leaves.name: SRS: reliability & availability
leaves.description: SRS system-quality topic covering reliability and availability requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: security
leaves.description: SRS system-quality topic covering security requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: maintainability
leaves.description: SRS system-quality topic covering maintainability requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: portability
leaves.description: SRS system-quality topic covering portability and installation requirements.

leaves.name: SRS: safety requirements
leaves.description: SRS section specifying safety requirements and hazard-related constraints where applicable.

leaves.name: SRS: operational / environmental
leaves.description: SRS topic covering the operating environment, modes of operation and site adaptation.

leaves.name: SRS: policy & regulation
leaves.description: SRS topic covering policies, regulations and legal requirements the system must satisfy.

leaves.name: SRS: verification & validation
leaves.description: SRS section defining how requirements will be verified — inspection, analysis, demonstration, test.
